title: Pinterest Stock Jumps 19% on Earnings and Revenue Beats, as Well as Solid Guidance
text: Pinterest's third-quarter performance exceeded market expectations across multiple metrics. Revenue reached $763.2 million, representing 11% year-over-year growth and surpassing Wall Street's consensus estimate of $743.4 million. The company's bottom-line performance was particularly impressive. Adjusted earnings per share jumped to $0.28, a 155% increase from the prior year, significantly outpacing analyst expectations of $0.20. On a GAAP basis, Pinterest flipped from a loss of $0.10 per share to a profit of $0.01 per share. Global monthly active users (MAUs) reached 482 million, up 8% year-over-year. Sequential growth from Q2 also proved solid at 4%. The U.S. and Canada segment, comprising 81% of total revenue, showed modest 1% user growth, while the rest-of-world segment expanded 12%. CEO Bill Ready noted that the company was finding our best product market fit in years, highlighting strong user engagement and improved advertising results. Management projected fourth-quarter revenue growth of 11% to 13% year-over-year, slightly exceeding analyst expectations of 11% growth at the midpoint.
